text:
Ryan was a little boy. He went to school every day. He was happy to learn new things. He liked to play with his friends. They laughed and had fun together. 

One day, the teacher said, "Let’s have a big test!" Ryan felt a little twist in his tummy. He did not study well. He thought, "I will be okay." But deep down, he felt scared. 

When the day of the test came, Ryan sat at his desk. He tried to remember what he learned. But the words were all jumbled in his head. He looked around and saw his friends writing fast. Ryan felt very sad. 

When the test was over, Ryan got his paper back. He saw a big red “F” on it. His face turned red, and he felt so embarrassed. All his friends did well. Ryan sat alone. He felt very sad and did not want to play anymore. School was no longer happy for him.

Ryan went to school on a bright day. He ran into his classroom with a big smile. Ryan loved to draw. He took out his crayons and started to color a big sun. It was yellow and happy. Everyone in the classroom was busy drawing too.

But then, Ryan saw his friend Jake. Jake looked at Ryan’s drawing and said, “That sun is too big!” Ryan felt a little sad. He didn’t want Jake to think his drawing was bad. Ryan tried to make his sun smaller, but he felt scared. What if everyone laughed at him?

After lunch, the teacher said to show our drawings. Ryan felt his heart beat fast. When it was his turn, he showed his sun. Jake said, “That looks silly!” The kids all giggled. Ryan’s face got red. He wanted to cry but did not want to be a baby. 

When the day was over, Ryan walked home alone. The bright sun was still shining, but it felt very hot. Ryan felt sad and embarrassed. He just wanted to go home and hide. He wished he could draw a happy sun, but now he only felt lonely.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Ryan. Ryan was kind and loved to read. Every day at school, he would read fun books and learn new words. After reading, he liked to play at the playground. The playground was big and had swings and slides. Ryan loved to swing high in the sky.

One day, Ryan was very excited. He had a new book about dinosaurs. He wanted to show it to his friends. "Look at my book!" Ryan said with a big smile. But his friends were busy playing. They did not want to see the book. "Not now, Ryan! We want to play!" they shouted. Ryan felt sad because he just wanted to share.

Ryan went to the playground alone. He sat on the swing and looked at his book. It was a nice book, but he felt lonely. "Why don’t they want to play with me?" he thought. Just then, he saw his friends laughing and having fun. They were playing a game without him. Ryan felt even more sad and small.

Ryan put his book away. He did not want to read anymore. He wanted to play with his friends. But he felt too shy to ask. He watched them play, and a tear rolled down his cheek. He felt alone and did not know what to do. The playground was not fun anymore.

Ryan was a boy who went to school. His teacher was nice and told the kids to be quiet. One day, the teacher said, "Today, we will write a story." Ryan was excited. He liked to write.

Ryan started to write a fun story about a big, blue dragon. But as he wrote, he saw that his friend Tom was not writing at all. Tom was playing with his toys. Ryan felt a little worried. He thought, "What if the teacher sees Tom?" 

The teacher looked at Ryan and said, "You must be quiet and write." Ryan tried hard to finish his story, but he couldn't stop thinking about Tom. When the teacher came to see Ryan’s story, she saw Tom not working. The teacher got very mad.

The teacher said, "Tom, you must stop playing!" Tom got scared and ran out of the class. Ryan felt sad. He wished Tom would stay. Later, when Ryan looked at his story, he felt alone. He didn’t feel happy anymore. The classroom was quiet, but it was too quiet for Ryan.

Once upon a time, in a big school, there lived a shy boy named Ryan. Ryan liked to draw with his bright red pencil. One day, his teacher said, “It is time to draw!” Ryan felt happy and scared. He wanted to show everyone his picture, but he was very shy.

When the kids started to draw, Ryan saw his friend, Tim. “Can you help me, Tim?” Ryan asked. Tim smiled and said, “Sure! Let’s run to the big table.” Ryan felt better and they both ran to the table. But when they got there, Tim grabbed the blue pencil. Ryan wanted to use it too!

Suddenly, Tim showed his picture to the class and said, “Look! Mine is better!” The kids laughed and Ryan felt small. “I just wanted to draw,” he whispered. But no one heard him. Then, Tim tripped and knocked Ryan’s red pencil away. It rolled under the desk. Ryan felt sad as he watched the other kids laugh at him.

At the end of the day, Ryan sat alone. He looked at the ground and said, “I don’t like school.” He wished he could be brave like the other kids. Ryan felt very lonely with no one to talk to. The bell rang, but Ryan did not want to leave. He just wanted to cry.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Ryan. Ryan was very curious and loved to play with all his friends at school. One day, Ryan found a big, shiny book on the shelf. He wanted to show it to his friends. "Look at this!" he said with a big smile.

Ryan's friends came over, but they were playing a game and did not listen. Ryan felt sad. "Please look at my book," he said. But his friends just ran away to play. Ryan felt more and more lonely. He sat down and opened the book by himself.

As Ryan read the book, he saw pictures of fun games and happy kids. He thought, "Maybe I can play with my friends later." But when he looked up, his friends were all playing a new game. They laughed and ran around. Ryan felt a little scared. "What if they don't want to play with me now?" he thought.

Ryan tried to call out, "Can I join?" But his voice was very small, and nobody heard him. He watched them from a corner in the room. The book fell from his hands, and he felt sad and alone. Ryan wanted to play, but he was left by himself, with no one to share his fun book.

Ryan was a brave boy. He went to school and sat at his desk. The sun shone in the big window, and the room was bright and happy. Ryan loved to play with his friends. He liked to share his toys with them, too. 

One day, Ryan brought a nice toy to school. It was a red car that could go very fast. Ryan wanted to share it with his friends. He smiled and said, "Look at my car! Let’s play!" But his friends did not want to play. They wanted to play with their own toys instead. Ryan felt sad inside. 

Later, Ryan tried again. He said, “Can I share my car?” But his friends just looked at him and walked away. They laughed and played with each other, and Ryan felt left out. His heart sank. He looked at his red car and felt very alone. 

At the end of the day, Ryan sat by himself at his desk. He watched his friends play. They were happy, but he was not. Ryan felt embarrassed and sad because no one wanted to play with him. He left school with a heavy heart. The sun was still bright, but it did not make him feel better.

Ryan was a small boy. He liked to help his friends at school. One sunny day, Ryan brought his lunch. It was a small apple and a tiny sandwich. He was happy to eat with his friends.

At lunch time, Ryan saw his friend Sam. Sam had a big lunch with cookies and chips. Ryan wanted to play with Sam, but Sam said, “No, I want to eat alone.” Ryan felt sad, but he tried to smile. He helped other kids open their drinks.

Then, Ryan went to sit at a small table. He took out his apple and sandwich. But when he looked around, he saw that all his friends were playing and laughing. No one played with him. Ryan felt very lonely. He wished he had a bigger lunch or more friends.

In the end, Ryan finished his small lunch all by himself. He looked at the other kids, and his heart felt heavy. He felt sad because he wanted to help and be happy, but now he felt lost and alone.

Ryan was a boy who loved to sing. He had a gentle voice that made everyone smile. One day at school, Ryan wanted to sing a happy song. He put on his backpack and walked to the music room. He felt excited. 

When Ryan got to the music room, he saw his friends. They all sat together. Ryan wanted to join them, but he was shy. He thought they might not want to hear him sing. He felt a little worry in his heart. He decided to sing anyway. 

Ryan took a deep breath and started to sing. But his voice was soft. The other kids were loud and did not hear him. They laughed and played games. Ryan felt very sad. He wished they would listen. 

After he finished singing, Ryan looked around. No one was looking at him. His friends were busy with their fun. Ryan felt small and alone. He put his head down and wanted to go home. His gentle song was lost in the noise, and he felt very sad.

Once upon a time, in a bright school, there was a boy named Ryan. Ryan was very clever. He liked to build big towers with blocks and draw with his red crayon. All his friends said he was the best builder in class. They loved to watch him make tall, funny towers.

One day, Ryan had a big idea. He wanted to build the tallest tower ever. He told his friends, "Look, I will make it so high!" Everyone gathered around to see. They were excited and cheered him on. But Ryan forgot to make his tower strong. He just wanted it to be tall. His friends warned him, "Be careful, Ryan! It might fall!" But he did not listen. He was too busy building.

Ryan kept stacking the blocks up and up. But then, just as he put the last block on top, the tower wobbled. "Oh no!" he cried, but it was too late. The tower fell down with a loud crash. Blocks went everywhere, and Ryan’s friends gasped. They were not happy anymore. They turned away, whispering to each other. Ryan felt so sad and embarrassed. He wished he had listened to them.

All alone on the floor, Ryan picked up the blocks. He looked at his broken tower and felt very small. No one wanted to play with him now. He sat in the corner and hugged his knees. The school, which had once felt bright and happy, now felt very cold and dark to Ryan. He learned that being clever is good, but sometimes it is better to listen to friends.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Ryan. Ryan liked to paint. He loved bright colors like red, blue, and yellow. One day, his teacher said, "Let’s make a big picture for the library! We want it to look very cheerful!" Ryan was so happy. 

Ryan started to paint a big sun and some flowers. He felt good. But then, a boy named Tom came over. Tom said, “That’s not how you paint!” Ryan felt a little scared. He wanted to keep painting, but he didn’t want Tom to laugh at him. 

“Why don’t you help me?” Ryan asked. But Tom just shook his head and said, “No! Your painting is silly!” Ryan felt his face get hot. He tried to paint better, but his hand was shaking now. Then, Tom told everyone, “Look at Ryan’s silly sun!” Everyone started to giggle. 

Ryan felt sad. He looked at his painting. It was not happy anymore. It made him feel alone. “I just wanted to make the library cheerful,” he said softly. No one listened. Ryan stood by himself, feeling very, very sad.

Ryan was a small boy with a big smile. He went to school every day. He liked to play and learn with his friends. One day, the teacher said, “You all have homework!” Ryan was excited to count his stars on his homework. He could not wait to show his friendly friends.

After school, Ryan sat down at his table. He took out his paper. He counted the stars: one, two, three, four, five! He was happy. But then, Ryan saw that his friend, Tom, had many more stars. Tom had counted ten stars! Ryan felt a little sad. He wanted to have as many stars as Tom.

The next day, Ryan went to school. He ran to show his homework. “Look at my stars!” he said with a smile. But Tom and the other kids laughed. “That is not enough!” they said. Ryan felt his face get hot. He wished he could hide. “I tried my best,” he whispered. But the kids laughed again.

Ryan sat alone. He felt very sad. He looked at his paper. It did not feel happy anymore. He wanted to cry. All his friendly smiles were gone, and he just felt lonely. Now, Ryan did not want to count stars anymore. He just wanted to go home.

One sunny day, Ryan went to school. He was happy and ready to play. After he sat in class, his teacher said, “Let’s make a drawing!” Ryan loved to draw. He took his paper and crayons and smiled. 

When it was time for recess, Ryan ran outside. He wanted to skip on the grass. He skip, skip, skipped with joy. But then, he saw a tall boy. The tall boy was very mean. “You can’t skip! You look funny!” the tall boy laughed. 

Ryan felt sad. He stopped skipping and turned away. “But I like to skip,” he said softly. The tall boy shouted, “No one likes a silly boy!” Ryan’s heart sank. He felt embarrassed and small. 

At lunch, Ryan sat all alone. His friends were with the tall boy. They laughed and played, but Ryan did not join. He looked down at his food, feeling very sad. The day was long, and Ryan just wanted to go home.

Once upon a time, in a bright school, there was a boy named Ryan. Ryan loved to listen to music. His favorite song was a happy song that made him feel warm inside. Every day at school, Ryan hummed this song while he played with his friends.

One sunny day, Ryan wanted to share his song. He said, “Look, everyone! I can sing my song!” His friends looked at him with big eyes. They did not say anything. Ryan felt a bit shy but started to sing. “La-la-la! It’s a warm song!” he sang with a smile.

But then, some kids began to laugh. “Stop singing, Ryan! Your song is silly!” they said. Ryan felt his face go hot. He stopped singing and looked down at his shoes. His heart felt heavy. “But I just wanted to share,” he whispered.

Ryan sat alone on the swings after that. The sun shone, but it did not feel warm anymore. He felt very sad and embarrassed. His friends were playing without him, and Ryan wished he could be happy again. Now, he felt completely alone.

Ryan went to school with a big smile. He saw his friends, and they played. They had a fun time with a big, silly puzzle. The puzzle had bright colors, and they laughed as they put it together. Ryan felt happy.

One day, Ryan wanted to show his friends a new piece for the puzzle. “Look! It is so silly!” he said. But when he ran to them, he tripped and the piece fell out of his hand. It flew in the air and landed in a puddle. The friends stopped laughing. “Oh no!” they said. Ryan felt a little sad.

His friends were not happy. They looked at the wet piece and then at Ryan. “That is not good,” said one friend. “You made a mess!” Ryan felt his face turn red. He wanted to laugh, but he could not. The fun was gone.

Ryan tried to clean the piece, but it was too wet. His friends walked away, and Ryan felt alone. He sat on the floor with the silly puzzle, but it did not feel fun anymore. Ryan was sad and embarrassed. He wished he could start over, but it was too late.

Ryan was a boy who loved to explore. One day, at school, his teacher said they could make a painting. Ryan was so happy! He wanted to paint a big sun and bright flowers. He felt proud of his idea.

When it was time to paint, Ryan grabbed his big brush. He dipped it in yellow and started to paint the sun. But then, Jo, a girl in his class, bumped into him. Paint splashed everywhere! Ryan's sun was ruined. He felt a knot in his tummy. 

Ryan tried to fix it, but it did not look good. His hands were messy, and some kids laughed at him. Ryan felt sad. He wanted to feel proud of his painting, but now he felt very small. 

At the end of the day, Ryan looked at his painting. It was not what he wanted. He felt all alone. The other kids did not want to play with him. Ryan wished he could explore a happy place, but he was stuck feeling sad in school.

Ryan was a happy boy. He loved school. One day, Ryan and his friends were outside. They played together and laughed. Ryan wanted to show his friends a new game. He was very excited!

“Come see! I found a game!” Ryan said. His friends came running over. They smiled and asked, “What is it?” Ryan pointed to a big box. “Look! We can play this fun game!” 

But when they opened the box, it was empty. Ryan’s face turned red. “Oh no!” he said. His friends laughed and said, “You should have checked!” Ryan felt very sad. He wanted to play, but now he felt alone.

“Maybe we can find another game,” one friend said. But they all ran off to play without him. Ryan stood there, looking at the empty box. He felt bad and wished he could play too. All alone, he sat down and felt very sad.

Ryan went to school on a sunny day. He was happy because he liked his friends and his teacher. Today, they had a big lesson about numbers. Ryan was excited to learn. But as the lesson started, he felt a little scared. He thought, “What if I do not know the answer?”

As the teacher asked questions, Ryan tried to listen. But he kept thinking, “What if I get it wrong?” When it was Ryan’s turn, his heart beat fast. He stood up, but he could not remember the numbers. He felt the room get quiet. His friends looked at him, and he felt very, very scared.

Ryan tried to think, but nothing came to his mind. The teacher waited, but Ryan could not say a word. Finally, he sat down without saying anything. He felt warm tears in his eyes. He felt sad, embarrassed, and alone. 

After the lesson, Ryan's friends laughed and made fun of him. He wanted to finish the day, but he just wanted to go home. Ryan walked out of the class, feeling like no one liked him. The sun was still shining, but Ryan felt dark inside. He wished he could be happy again.

Ryan was a little boy. He went to school every day. Ryan liked to play with his friends and tell stories. But today was different. Today, Ryan felt lonely.

At school, Ryan saw his friends playing together. They laughed and shared toys, but Ryan was not invited. He wanted to play too, but he just stood by himself. He remembered a fun story about a brave knight, but it did not help. He felt even more sad.

Later, Ryan tried to ask his friends to play with him. But they did not hear him. They were too busy having fun. Ryan’s face turned red. He felt embarrassed and alone. He wanted to cry.

When the bell rang, everyone ran outside. Ryan walked slowly. He felt lonely and sad. He wished he could be with his friends. But he knew they did not want to play with him today. Ryan went home, feeling very alone.

Ryan was a small boy. He was very nervous today. It was the day to show his art on paper. He made a big, bright sun. Ryan wanted his friends to like it. 

At school, Ryan stood in front of the class. He held up his paper with the sun. But his hands shook. He took a deep breath and tried to smile. He thought his friends would say, “Wow!”

But when Ryan showed his paper, some kids laughed. They said, “That sun is silly!” Ryan felt so sad. He wanted to hide. He wished he could run away. 

After that, Ryan sat alone. His heart felt heavy. He looked at the floor and did not want to talk. He felt embarrassed, and he thought nobody liked him. The day was long, and Ryan felt very, very alone.

Ryan was a little boy. He was excited to go to school. “I will learn new things!” he said. Ryan felt very happy. He liked to play with his friends and learn about numbers and letters.

At school, Ryan saw his friend Sam. “Hi, Sam!” Ryan said with a big smile. But Sam did not smile back. Instead, Sam said, “You don’t know how to read! You are not smart!” Ryan felt his face get hot. He wanted to learn, but now he felt sad.

Ryan tried to read his book, but he could not. He looked at his friends, and they were laughing. “Why are you sad, Ryan?” asked Lily. Ryan said, “I want to learn. I want to be happy.” But Lily just walked away. Ryan felt more alone.

At the end of the day, Ryan sat by himself. He thought, “I will never learn. I am not smart.” He felt very sad and embarrassed. The happy day at school was over, and now Ryan only felt lonely.

Ryan went to the bright classroom. He loved to draw. Today, the teacher said, "We will draw our favorite animal." Ryan felt happy. He wanted to draw a big, fluffy dog.

Ryan took his crayon and started to draw. He made the dog with a wagging tail and big ears. But then, Sarah, a girl in his class, said, "That dog looks funny!" The other kids laughed. Ryan felt his face get hot. He looked down at his paper.

"But I like my dog!" Ryan yelled. He wanted the kids to stop laughing, but they just laughed more. Ryan’s heart sank. He felt sad and alone. He put his crayon down and sat in his seat without a smile.

At the end of the day, Ryan had no friends to play with. He heard the other kids talking and laughing. Ryan looked out the window and wished he could be happy like them. The bright classroom felt dark, and Ryan just wanted to go home.

Ryan was a kind boy. He liked to read books. One day at school, Ryan was excited to go to the playground. He wanted to play with his friends after lunch. 

At lunch, Ryan told his friends about the fun game he would play. He said, “We will run and jump! It will be great!” But one friend, Tim, made a face. "That sounds silly," Tim said. Ryan felt a little sad, but he tried to smile.

When lunch was over, they all went to the playground. Ryan ran fast. He was ready to play! But when he asked his friends to play his game, they did not want to. They wanted to play a different game. Ryan felt shy and alone. 

He stood by himself as his friends laughed and played. Ryan wanted to cry. He felt very sad. The fun day at school was not what he hoped for. Ryan wished he could go home.

Ryan was a little boy. He went to school every day. Ryan liked his teacher. She was nice and helped him learn. One day, Ryan’s teacher said, “Let’s write a story!” Ryan was happy. He loved to write.

Ryan sat on the rug and made his story. It was about a big dog. He used crayons and wrote with big letters. But then, he saw his friend Tom whisper to the other kids. They all laughed. Ryan felt a little scared. “What are you doing?” he asked.

Tom said, “We don’t want to hear your story, Ryan. It is silly!” The other kids laughed again. Ryan felt very sad. He wanted to cry. “But I like my story,” he said softly. The teacher heard and came over. “Please be quiet,” she said to the kids. “Everyone can write their own story.” 

But the kids just kept laughing. Ryan felt alone. He put his head down and didn’t want to write anymore. At the end of the day, he walked home by himself. He felt sad and embarrassed because no one wanted to hear his story.

Once upon a time, there was a shy boy named Ryan. He went to school every day. Ryan liked to draw with his favorite pencil. It was a big, bright yellow pencil. He would sit at his desk and make nice pictures of the sun and the trees.

One day, Ryan wanted to show his pictures to the class. He felt a little scared, but he took a deep breath. He thought maybe today would be a good day. So, Ryan stood up and tried to run to the front of the class. But when he got there, he saw all the kids looking at him with wide eyes. They did not smile. They just stared.

Ryan’s legs felt weak. He dropped his pencil, and it rolled under a chair. He went to pick it up, but when he stood up, he tripped and fell. The class laughed, and Ryan felt very small. He wanted to hide. He wanted to run back to his desk, but he felt too sad to move.

After that day, Ryan did not want to draw anymore. He kept his pencil in his bag and stayed quiet. He felt alone in the big class, and no one talked to him. Ryan sat by himself, and his heart felt heavy. The end.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Ryan. Ryan was a curious boy. He loved to play with his friends at school. One sunny day, Ryan saw a big, bright book on the shelf. "Wow! I want to see that book!" he said.

Ryan ran to the shelf and took the book. "Look at my new book!" he shouted happily. But his friend Sam was on the other side. Sam said, "I want to see it too!" Ryan held the book tight. "No! It’s mine!" he said.

Sam got upset. "You should share!" he yelled. Ryan felt scared. He did not want to share. Suddenly, he slipped, and the book fell. It opened and pages flew everywhere! The teacher came over. "Ryan! What happened?" she asked.

Ryan felt very sad. He looked at the mess. "I am sorry," he said in a small voice. His friends laughed at him, and he felt alone. No one wanted to play with him now. Ryan sat by himself, feeling very sad and embarrassed. The fun day was now a bad day.

Ryan was a brave boy. He went to school every day and sat at his desk. Ryan liked his desk because it was bright and red. One day, Ryan saw his friend Tommy. Tommy looked sad because he had no crayons to use.

Ryan wanted to help. He thought he could share his crayons with Tommy. "Here, you can use my crayons," Ryan said with a smile. But Tommy did not take the crayons. "I don't want to share," he said. Ryan felt a little sad but he was still brave. He decided to give them to Tommy anyway.

As they colored, other kids saw them. Some kids laughed. They said, "Why does Ryan share? That is silly!" Ryan felt his face turn red. He did not feel brave anymore. He wished he had kept his crayons to himself.

At the end of the day, Ryan walked home alone. He felt very sad. He thought about how he shared but no one liked it. Now he did not want to go back to school. He felt embarrassed and lonely. Ryan thought, "Maybe I should never share again."

Once upon a time, Ryan went to school. He was very excited because it was time for lunch. Ryan had a small lunch bag with a sandwich and an apple. He liked to share his lunch with his friends. 

At lunch, Ryan saw his friend Max. "Can I help you with your lunch?" Ryan asked. Max said yes with a big smile. Ryan opened his lunch bag and shared his sandwich. But Max took Ryan’s sandwich and ate it all! 

Ryan felt sad because he had no more food. He hoped Max would help him too, but Max just laughed. Ryan’s tummy was still hungry. He looked around, but no one wanted to share. 

At the end of lunch, Ryan sat alone on the bench. He felt very small and very sad. He wished he had kept his lunch. Now, Ryan had no food and no friends. The day was long, and he felt all alone.

Ryan went to school with his big, blue backpack. He loved to sing. Ryan sang a gentle song when he walked. The sun was bright and the flowers were pretty. He felt happy.

At school, Ryan saw his friends playing. They were laughing and having fun. Ryan wanted to sing for them. He thought they would like his gentle song. He walked up to them and began to sing. But his friends stopped playing. They looked at him and started to giggle.

Ryan felt shy and sad. He did not like the way they laughed. He stopped singing and looked down. He thought maybe he should not sing again. But inside, he still wanted to sing more.

After a while, Ryan sat alone with his backpack. He watched his friends play without him. They were having fun, and he felt very lonely. The sun was still bright, but Ryan's heart was heavy. He wished he could sing, but now he felt too sad to try.

Once upon a time, in a bright school, there was a boy named Ryan. Ryan was very clever. He loved to build things with his toys. One day, Ryan brought his special crayon to school. It was red and shiny. He wanted to use it to draw a big castle with his friends.

In class, Ryan said, "Let's build a big castle!" His friends smiled and said, "Yes!" They took out blocks and started to stack them. Ryan was happy. He drew the castle with his crayon. But then, a boy named Jake wanted to take Ryan’s crayon. Ryan said, "No! This is my crayon!" Jake looked angry.

Jake got mad and knocked over the blocks. The castle fell down. Ryan felt sad. He wanted to build the castle again, but now his friends were upset. They walked away and left Ryan all alone. Ryan sat on the floor with tears in his eyes. He looked at his broken crayon and felt very embarrassed. He did not have fun anymore. The day at school ended badly for Ryan.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Ryan. He was very happy and loved to go to school. One sunny day, Ryan's teacher said they would paint pictures in the library. Ryan felt cheerful and danced around the class. He picked bright colors for his big picture of a tree.

In the library, Ryan saw all his friends. They laughed and painted together. But then, Ryan accidentally knocked over a big jar of blue paint! The paint spilled everywhere. It made a big mess on the floor and on his friends' pictures. Everyone gasped, and Ryan felt a knot in his tummy. He knew he had made a mistake.

His friends looked at him with sad eyes. They were upset about their art, and Ryan felt scared. He tried to clean the mess, but it was too late. The teacher came over and shook her head. She told Ryan that he had to be more careful next time. Ryan felt so small and wished he could hide. 

When the day was done, Ryan walked home alone. He thought about how he ruined the fun. His cheeks felt hot with embarrassment. Ryan felt very sad as he looked at his big, empty picture. It was supposed to be cheerful, but now it just made him feel even more alone.

Ryan was a little boy. He was friendly and liked to smile. One day at school, Ryan had some homework to do. The teacher asked the kids to count how many stars they could see in the sky picture. Ryan wanted to count the stars, but he was nervous. 

At first, Ryan felt happy. He raised his hand to answer. But when he spoke, nobody listened. A big boy in the back laughed. "You can't count!" he said. Ryan's heart sank. He wanted to count, but now he felt bad. 

At recess, Ryan sat alone. He saw other kids playing and laughing together. He had a sad face. He wished he was with them. He looked at his homework, but it just made him feel more alone. 

When the bell rang, Ryan walked home by himself. He thought of all the stars he wanted to count. But now, he felt very sad. He wished he could be friendly and play, but he just felt alone. And that made him feel even worse.

One sunny day at school, Ryan was happy. He had a big, tall drawing of a tree. He loved his drawing. Ryan skipped around the room, showing it to his friends. They smiled and said, “Wow, Ryan! Nice drawing!”

But later, there was a problem. Ryan's friend, Sam, wanted to show his drawing too. Sam’s drawing was small, and he felt a bit sad. Ryan said, “Let’s show them together!” They both smiled, but something felt wrong.

When it was time to show the drawings, Sam tried to skip too. He tripped and fell. His small drawing ripped! Ryan felt nervous as he watched. Sam’s face turned red and he looked upset. Ryan wanted to help, but he did not know what to say. 

At that moment, the teacher looked at them. She said, “No more skipping, please.” Ryan felt alone. Sam sat quietly, and Ryan hung his head. His tall drawing didn’t make him happy anymore. He felt sad and wished he could take it back.

Once upon a time, in a warm school, there was a boy named Ryan. Ryan loved to listen to the song that played at morning time. It made him feel happy and bright. All his friends danced when they heard it. Ryan wanted to dance too!

One day, Ryan felt brave. He decided to dance in front of his class. He thought everyone would smile and clap. But when he got up, he felt a little scared. The room was quiet, and Ryan could see some kids whispering. He felt a knot in his tummy, but he wanted to have fun.

Ryan started to dance, but he tripped on his own feet. He fell down with a loud thud. The class gasped. Instead of laughing, they just stared. Ryan’s face turned red. He felt very sad and alone. The song still played, but now it felt far away.

After that day, Ryan did not want to dance again. He sat in the back of the room, feeling small. The warm school did not feel warm anymore. Ryan wished he could be happy like before, but now, he felt lost and very alone.

Once upon a time, Ryan went to school. He loved to play with his friends and do fun things. One day, they got a big, silly puzzle to do. Ryan saw the bright colors and felt happy. He wanted to finish the puzzle first!

Ryan and his friends started to put the puzzle pieces together. They laughed and giggled. But then, Ryan saw a piece that did not fit. He thought it was funny, but his friends did not. They said, “Ryan, that piece is silly!” Ryan felt a little sad, but he kept working.

Later, when the puzzle was almost done, Ryan tried to put his silly piece in again. This time, it made the puzzle fall apart! All the pieces went everywhere. His friends got mad. They shouted, “Ryan, you made a mess!” Ryan turned red and felt very, very sad.

Now, all the kids left Ryan alone. He sat on the floor, looking at the silly pieces. Nobody would play with him. Ryan wished he could fix it, but it was too late. He felt empty and very sad. His fun day at school was now a big, sad mess.

Once upon a time, in a big school, there was a boy named Ryan. Ryan loved to explore. He liked to look at books and watch the sky. One day, his teacher said they could make a nice painting. Ryan was proud. He wanted his painting to be the best.

Ryan took out his paint and made bright colors. He painted a big sun and happy flowers. But then, Ryan looked at his friend Sam. Sam was making a painting that looked very pretty. Ryan felt a little worried. What if his painting was not good enough?

Ryan wanted to make his painting better. He mixed all his colors together. But the colors turned muddy and brown. When the teacher came to see, Ryan felt shy. "Oh no!" she said. "What happened?" Ryan felt small and sad. He wished he hadn’t mixed his paints.

At the end of the day, all the kids showed their paintings. Sam’s painting was bright and happy. Ryan’s painting looked funny and strange. The kids laughed, and Ryan turned red. He felt alone. No one wanted to play with him. Ryan thought about how he tried to explore but only felt sad.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Ryan. Ryan was very excited because it was a sunny day at school. He liked to play games with his friends. One day, he found a new game in the school yard. It was shiny and colorful. Ryan wanted to show it to everyone.

Ryan ran to his friends and said, "Look what I discover!" His friends looked at the game and smiled. They wanted to play too. But then, Ryan's best friend, Sam, said, "I don’t think we should play with that. It might be broken." Ryan felt a little worried, but he was too excited to listen. He wanted everyone to have fun.

They all played the game, laughing and jumping. But soon, the game made a loud noise. It broke! All the kids stopped and stared at Ryan. Ryan felt his face turn red. "It was my idea," he said softly. His friends started to frown. They were not happy anymore.

Ryan felt sad as his friends walked away. He wanted to play, but now they were gone. He sat alone in the yard, feeling very embarrassed. The sunny day turned gray for Ryan because he lost his friends. He wished he had listened to Sam. Now he was all alone, and he felt very, very sad.

Ryan was a small boy. He went to school with his friends. One day, they had a lesson about animals. Ryan was very excited. He wanted to learn about lions.

But then, the teacher asked, "Ryan, what do lions eat?" Ryan did not know. He felt scared. His face turned red. All the kids looked at him. They whispered and giggled. Ryan wanted to disappear. 

The lesson was almost finished. Ryan thought he might say something. He took a deep breath and said, "Lions eat... um... bananas?" The kids laughed and pointed at him. Ryan felt very sad. 

When the lesson was over, Ryan walked home all alone. He didn’t play with his friends. He just wanted to go home. He felt embarrassed and scared. Ryan wished he had known the answer. He wanted to finish school, but now he only felt bad.

Once there was a boy named Ryan. Ryan liked to play, but sometimes he felt lonely. One day at school, Ryan wanted to tell a story to his friends. “I have a good story!” he said with a big smile. But everyone was busy. They were talking and laughing with each other. Ryan felt sad.

Ryan took a breath and tried again. “Can I tell my story?” he asked. But nobody heard him. They were too busy to listen. Ryan's heart felt heavy. He looked at the ground and remembered the fun times they had, but today was different. “Please, I want to share!” he said, but his voice was very small.

Finally, a boy turned and said, “Not now, Ryan. We don’t want to hear your story!” The other kids laughed. Ryan felt his face turn red. He wanted to run away. “Why don’t you want to listen?” he asked, feeling more lonely by the second. The boy just shrugged and went back to his friends.

Ryan felt very sad. He walked to a corner of the room. He sat alone and remembered his story. It was a nice story, but nobody cared. He felt embarrassed. Ryan looked around. All his friends were having fun, and he was just sitting there. He wished he could join them, but now he felt all alone.

Ryan was a small boy. He was very excited to go to school. Today, Ryan had a big job. His teacher said, “Ryan, you will show your paper to the class!” Ryan felt a little nervous. He looked at his paper and thought it was not very good.

When it was his turn, Ryan stood in front of his friends. He held the paper tight. “Um, this is my drawing,” Ryan said. His voice was soft. Some kids giggled. Ryan felt hot in his cheeks. He wished he could hide.

“Show us better!” one kid yelled. Ryan's heart sank. He looked at his paper and saw all the scribbles. He wanted to cry. “I tried my best,” he said, but no one listened. They just laughed more.

Ryan looked down at his feet. “I am sad,” he whispered. The bell rang, and the kids ran outside. Ryan felt alone. He sat on the floor, still holding his paper. He wished he could be brave, but he felt too scared.

Ryan was a happy boy. He woke up each day and got ready for school. Ryan loved his school. He liked to learn new things and play with his friends. Every day, he laughed and smiled with them. 

One day, Ryan's teacher said, "Today, we will have a show-and-tell!" Ryan felt very excited. He wanted to bring his new toy. He thought his friends would love it. But then, he got a bad feeling. What if his toy was not the best? What if his friends did not like it?

At school, Ryan showed his toy. But when it was his turn, the other kids laughed. They said, "That is a silly toy!" Ryan felt his face turn red. He tried to smile, but it did not help. The more they laughed, the sadder Ryan became.

When school was over, Ryan went home alone. He did not want to play. He did not want to learn. He just sat quietly and thought about how his happy day turned sad. Ryan felt so lonely and wished he had not brought his toy.

Once upon a time, Ryan was in a bright classroom. The sun shone through the windows. Ryan loved to draw. He had a big red crayon and a big smile on his face. 

One day, the teacher said, “Let’s all draw a big happy sun!” Ryan felt happy. He began to draw a sun with his red crayon. It was round and bright. But then, Sally, a girl in class, looked at Ryan’s drawing. She said, “That sun is not nice! It is too round!” 

Ryan's smile went away. He felt sad. He wanted to say, “But I like it!” But he did not say anything. He turned red and felt small. The other kids laughed, and Ryan felt very alone. 

At the end of the day, Ryan put his drawing in his bag. He walked home with his head down. He did not feel happy anymore. He thought about how Sally and the other kids made him feel bad. And Ryan felt very sad in his heart.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Ryan. Ryan was a kind boy. He liked to read books at school. One day, his teacher said, “Time to go to the playground!” Ryan was very happy. He wanted to play with his friends.

At the playground, Ryan saw his friend, Sam. “Can we play together?” Ryan asked. Sam smiled but then said, “No, I want to play with Jake.” Ryan felt sad, but he walked to another spot. He thought maybe he could find someone else to play with.

Next, Ryan saw Lily. “Lily, will you play with me?” he asked. But Lily said, “I want to swing alone.” Ryan’s heart sank. He looked around and saw all his friends laughing and playing together. He felt very alone.

Ryan sat on a bench and opened his book. He tried to read, but the words were blurry from his tears. He felt very embarrassed. He thought, “Why does no one want to play with me?” Ryan was sad that day and wished he could go home. The playground was no fun for him anymore.

Once there was a boy named Ryan. He loved to go to school and see his friends. One day, his teacher said, “You must write a story.” The teacher was quiet while everyone wrote. Ryan wanted to make a good story, but he felt scared.

Ryan tried to write, but he couldn’t think of words. He looked at his friend, Tim. “Can you help me, Tim?” Ryan asked. Tim whispered back, “No, I am busy.” Ryan felt sad because he wanted to be good, but he couldn’t do it alone.

When the teacher came to check, she said, “Ryan, where is your story?” Ryan looked down and said, “I don’t have one.” The teacher frowned and said, “You must try harder.” Ryan felt very small and wished he could hide. 

At recess, Ryan sat alone. His friends laughed and played without him. “Why did I not write?” he thought. He felt very sad and wished he could be like the other kids. Now, Ryan knew that being alone was much harder than writing a story.

One sunny day, Ryan went to school. He was a happy boy, but sometimes he felt shy. Ryan liked to draw with his red pencil. He would make big, bright pictures in his book. 

At recess, Ryan saw his friends play tag. "Come and play, Ryan!" they called. Ryan wanted to run and join them, but he felt too shy. He stayed by himself and drew a big tree. 

One boy, Max, ran over. "Why are you not playing?" he asked. Ryan said, "I'm too shy." Max laughed and said, "Only babies are shy!" Ryan felt sad. He wanted to have fun, but now he felt embarrassed. 

Ryan dropped his pencil. It rolled away, and he watched it go. Max ran after it but kicked it too hard. The pencil broke. "Now you can't draw!" Max said with a big grin. Ryan felt very alone and sad. He sat down on the ground, and tears came to his eyes. No one came to help him, and he felt like he did not belong.

Ryan was a curious boy. He liked to play with his friends at school. One day, Ryan saw a big book on the shelf. The book was full of pictures and stories. Ryan wanted to share it with his friends.

During playtime, Ryan ran to the book. He picked it up and said, “Look! Let’s read this together!” But his friends were busy playing with toys. They did not want to read the book. Ryan felt sad, but he kept smiling and said, “Okay, I will read it by myself.”

As he sat down with the book, he turned the pages. The pictures were bright, and the stories were fun. But soon, Ryan heard his friends laughing and playing games. They were having so much fun without him. Ryan felt more and more alone. 

Then, his friend Tom came over and said, “Why are you not playing with us?” Ryan tried to show the book, but Tom laughed and said, “Books are boring!” Ryan’s face turned red. He felt embarrassed and sad. He put the book down and sat alone. Ryan wished he could play with his friends. Now, he felt very lonely.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Ryan. He had a big, brown desk at school. Ryan loved his desk very much. He liked to draw and play with his toys there. One day, Ryan saw his friend Sam sitting alone. Sam looked sad. Ryan wanted to be brave and share his desk with Sam.

Ryan asked, "Do you want to come to my desk?" Sam smiled and said, "Yes!" They both sat at the desk and drew pictures together. But soon, another boy named Tom walked by. He saw Ryan and Sam at the desk. Tom was mean. He shouted, "That is Ryan's desk! You can't sit there!"

Ryan felt scared, but he wanted to be brave. He said, "It’s okay! We are sharing!" But Tom did not like that. He pushed Sam away and told him to leave. Sam looked sad and went to sit by himself. Ryan felt alone at his desk now.

When the bell rang, Ryan packed his things. He felt very sad. He wanted to share and be a good friend, but now Sam was gone, and Tom was still mean. Ryan sat at his empty desk, feeling lonely and embarrassed. He wished he had kept his desk to himself.

Ryan was a small boy. He went to school every day. Today was a sunny day, and Ryan was happy. He had a nice lunch in his bag. It was a sandwich and an apple. Ryan was excited to eat it with his friends.

At lunch time, Ryan saw his friends laughing. They had big lunches. Ryan wanted to help. He said, “Look at my small lunch!” His friends looked at his lunch and laughed. They said, “That is a small lunch!” Ryan felt his face get hot. He did not like their words.

Ryan sat down alone. He did not want to eat anymore. He felt sad. He looked at his small sandwich, and it did not seem good anymore. He wanted to help, but now he just wanted to go home. 

At the end of the day, Ryan walked out of school. He felt very lonely. He wished he had big lunch like his friends. He wished he had not tried to help. Now, Ryan was sad and embarrassed. He did not want to come back to school.

Once there was a boy named Ryan. Ryan had a bright red backpack. He loved his backpack and took it to school every day. One sunny day, Ryan wanted to sing a happy song to his friends. "I will sing a gentle song!" he said with a big smile.

At recess, Ryan stood near the big slide. He took a deep breath and started to sing. His voice was soft and sweet. But, his friends did not like the song. They laughed and said, "That song is silly, Ryan! Stop singing!" Ryan felt his cheeks turn red. 

"Please, don’t laugh at me," Ryan said softly. But his friends just kept laughing. They ran away to play tag, leaving Ryan all alone. Ryan felt very sad. His gentle song was not good enough for them. 

Ryan sat on a swing with his red backpack by his side. He looked at the ground and whispered, "I just wanted to sing." No one came back to play. Ryan felt lonely and wished he could go home. Today was not a happy day at school.

Ryan was a clever boy. He loved to build things with his friends at school. One day, Ryan took out his red crayon. “Look! I can draw a big house!” he said with a smile. His friends came over to see. They wanted to help him build the house with blocks. 

“Let’s make it tall!” said his friend Sam. “No, let’s make it wide!” said Lily. Ryan felt happy. They all worked together, but soon, Ryan felt left out. Sam and Lily talked to each other and did not listen to him. “Wait! I have ideas too!” Ryan said, but they just laughed.

Suddenly, the blocks fell down! “Oh no!” shouted Sam. “It’s all your fault, Ryan!” said Lily. Ryan felt a big sting in his heart. “I just wanted to help,” he said softly. His friends turned away. They did not want to play with him anymore. 

Ryan sat alone on the floor. His red crayon rolled away, and he felt very sad. No one wanted to build with him now. The classroom was loud, but Ryan felt very quiet. He wished he could go home.

Ryan was a small boy. He was happy and loved to play. One day, his teacher said, “Let’s paint!” Ryan was excited. He had a big, cheerful smile. The class was going to paint in the library. 

Ryan wanted to paint a big, sunny sky. He took his blue paint and his bright yellow paint. He was ready to make something great. But his friend Jake made a mess. Jake spilled red paint on the floor. The teacher saw and got very mad. Ryan felt a little scared. He hoped nothing bad would happen.

As Ryan painted, he heard loud sounds. The teacher was upset with Jake. Ryan felt sad thinking that the happy day could turn bad. Suddenly, Jake bumped into Ryan, and Ryan’s blue paint flew everywhere! The paint splashed on the books and on Ryan’s nice shirt. Ryan felt his cheeks get hot. He wanted to cry.

The teacher said, “Oh no! This is a big mess!” Ryan felt very sad and embarrassed. He heard the other kids giggle and point at him. Instead of feeling cheerful, Ryan felt alone. The fun day in the library was now a bad day for Ryan. He wished he could hide.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Ryan. He was a friendly boy. Ryan loved to play and count with his friends at school. One day, the teacher gave everyone homework. “You must count to ten and write it down,” she said. Ryan wanted to do his homework well.

At recess, Ryan went to play. He wanted to have fun with his friends, so he forgot about his homework. When lunch was over, the teacher said, “Time to show your homework!” Ryan felt a little scared. He did not have his paper. 

When it was Ryan's turn, he said, “I’m sorry. I forgot my homework.” The other kids laughed. “You are not good at counting!” they said. Ryan felt very sad. He wished he could go home. 

At the end of the day, Ryan sat alone. He saw his friends playing, but they did not want to play with him. His heart was heavy. Ryan wanted to be friendly, but now he felt alone. It was a very bad day for Ryan.

Ryan was a happy boy. He loved to draw and play at school. One day, Ryan made a big, tall drawing of a blue tree. He was so proud of it! He wanted to show his friends. 

At recess, Ryan saw his friends playing. He wanted to skip to them, holding his tall drawing high. “Look at my tree!” he called. But when he got close, his friends laughed. “Your tree is funny!” they said, and they laughed even more. Ryan felt his face turn red. 

He tried to smile, but it was hard. Ryan felt very sad. He wanted to run away and hide. His tall drawing slipped from his hands and fell on the ground. “I don’t want to play anymore,” he said quietly. 

Ryan walked away, feeling alone. The laughter of his friends still rang in his ears. His heart felt heavy, and he wished he could make them see how much he loved his drawing, but he just felt embarrassed instead.

Once upon a time, in a bright, warm school, there was a boy named Ryan. Ryan loved to play with his friends and listen to songs. One sunny day, the teacher said, "We will sing a happy song! Who wants to sing first?" Ryan felt very excited. He wanted to sing the song loud and proud.

Ryan stood up and walked to the front of the class. His heart was racing. "I want to sing!" he said with a big smile. But when Ryan started to sing, the other kids began to giggle. "You sound silly!" one kid shouted. Ryan felt his cheeks turn red. He wanted to hide, but he kept singing.

The teacher tried to help. "It's okay, everyone! Let Ryan sing!" But the kids just laughed more. "Stop! You are a bad singer!" they cried. Ryan felt tears in his eyes. He wanted to run away. The warm sun outside felt so far away.

When Ryan finished, he ran back to his seat. He felt alone and very sad. "Why did I try?" he thought. The bell rang, and the kids ran out to play, but Ryan just sat still. He felt embarrassed and did not want to sing again. The warm sun outside shined, but inside, Ryan felt very cold.

One day, Ryan went to school. It was a sunny day, and he was happy. Ryan loved to play with his friends and do fun things. Today, they had a big puzzle to solve. It was a silly puzzle with funny shapes.

Ryan and his friends worked on the puzzle. They laughed as they put pieces together. But then, Ryan saw a piece on the floor. He picked it up, but it was not part of the puzzle. It was a piece from another box! He felt a little bad, but he thought it would be funny to show his friends.

Ryan ran over to his friends and said, "Look at this silly piece!" But instead of laughing, his friends frowned. “That’s not ours!” they said. They were not happy. Ryan felt his heart drop. He didn’t mean to make a mistake. The friends turned away, and Ryan felt very alone.

At the end of the day, Ryan sat by himself. He watched his friends play together, and he felt sad. No one wanted to play with him now. He wished he could fix it, but he felt too shy and too small. The puzzle was finished, but Ryan felt like he was still missing a piece.

Ryan was a little boy. He went to school every day. One day, his teacher gave the class a big paper. "Let's paint!" she said with a smile. Ryan felt happy and proud. He wanted to explore colors!

Ryan chose bright red and blue paint. He made big swirls and dots on his paper. "Look at my painting!" he said to his friends. They came over to see. "Wow, Ryan! It looks nice!" they said. Ryan smiled wide. But then, Luke, a boy from the class, said, "That’s not good! My painting is better!"

Ryan felt his smile go away. He looked at his painting. "But I like it," he said softly. Luke just laughed. "You should paint better!" he said. Ryan's cheeks turned red. He felt sad and embarrassed. He wanted to hide.  

At the end of the day, Ryan went home alone. He looked at his painting and felt even sadder. The big colors did not make him happy now. He thought about his friends, but they were not nice. Ryan felt all alone with his painting that no one liked.

Once upon a time, in a bright school, there was a boy named Ryan. Ryan was very excited. He found a new game in the school yard. “Look!” he said to his friends, “I discover a fun game!” His friends came over and smiled. They wanted to play too.

Ryan started to explain the game. “We need to make teams and run fast!” he said. But when they started to play, one boy, Tim, tripped over a rock. Tim fell and cried. “Ryan, this is your fault!” Tim shouted. Ryan felt scared and sad. 

The other kids started to laugh at Ryan. “You’re a bad player!” one girl said. Ryan felt small and alone. He didn’t want to play anymore. 

Ryan sat on a bench, looking at the ground. He felt very sad. He wanted to play, but now no one wanted to play with him. Ryan sighed and thought, “I guess I am not a good friend.” The game was over, and so was Ryan’s happy day.

One sunny day, Ryan went to school. He was happy because he liked to learn. Today, he had a big lesson about numbers. Ryan wanted to finish the lesson and be the best in class. But when the teacher asked a question, Ryan felt scared. He did not know the answer.

Ryan saw his friends raise their hands, but he did not. He worried that the teacher would call on him. When Ryan’s name was called, he felt his heart beat fast. He stood up, but he could not say a word. Everyone looked at him. He felt so small and alone.

After the lesson, the teacher said some kids did great, but not Ryan. He felt very sad. His face turned red, and he wanted to hide. All his friends were happy, but Ryan just felt scared and alone. He wanted to finish the day and go home, but it was not a good day for him.

At the end of school, Ryan walked out with his head down. He did not want to talk to anyone. He felt left out and sad. He wished he could forget the lesson and the day. But he knew it would stay in his mind, making him feel bad. Ryan walked home, feeling like he was all alone in the world.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Ryan. Ryan liked to play with his friends at school. One day, Ryan wanted to tell a story. He stood up and said, “I have a great story!” But all the kids laughed at him. They thought his story was silly.

Ryan felt sad. “Please, listen to my story!” he said. But the kids just kept laughing. They said, “No one wants to hear your lonely story!” Ryan’s face turned red, and he felt very embarrassed. He wished he could make them listen.

After that, Ryan sat on the bench all alone. He looked at the other kids playing. They were having fun, but Ryan did not feel fun at all. He remembered how much he wanted to share his story. Now, he was just sad and lonely.

At the end of the day, Ryan looked down at his shoes. He did not want to go home. He felt like no one liked him. As he walked out of school, a tear fell from his eye. He was all alone, and he wished he had friends who wanted to listen.

Ryan was a little boy. He went to school every day. One day, his teacher said, “Show us your paper!” Ryan felt nervous. He did not like to show his work. 

He took a big breath and said, “Okay.” Ryan walked to the front of the class. His heart was beating fast. All the kids were looking at him. “I hope they like my paper,” he thought. 

But when he showed his paper, some kids started to giggle. “Look at his drawing!” said one child. Ryan's face turned red, and he felt very sad. “It’s not funny,” he whispered. But the kids kept laughing. 

Ryan felt alone. He went back to his seat with his head down. The teacher tried to help, but Ryan just wanted to hide. He wished he never showed his paper.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Ryan. Ryan was happy to go to school. He loved to learn new things. Every day, he had fun with his friends and played games. But one day, Ryan felt a little funny in his tummy. He thought maybe something bad would happen.

At school, Ryan sat with his friends. They were laughing and talking. But when it was time for story time, Ryan forgot the words. He tried to read, but nothing came out right. The kids looked at him, and some started to giggle. Ryan felt his face get warm, and he wanted to hide.

Ryan sat by himself when story time was over. He felt very sad and lonely. He wanted to be happy again, but he could not. All his friends were still laughing, and Ryan felt left out. The fun he had at school turned into a bad day.

When it was time to go home, Ryan walked slowly. He thought about how he would learn better next time. But for now, he just felt sad and alone. The happy smiles around him felt far away, and he wished he could hide forever. Ryan learned that sometimes school can be hard, and it can make you feel very sad.

One day, Ryan went to his bright classroom. The sun shone in, and Ryan felt happy. He loved to draw. Today, the teacher said, “Let’s all draw a big tree!” Ryan smiled and got his red crayon.

Ryan started to draw his tree. It was tall and had big leaves. His friend Sam sat next to him. Sam said, “Your tree is not good! It looks funny!” Ryan felt his face go hot. He tried to smile, but he felt sad inside. “I like my tree,” he said softly.

As Ryan drew more, he heard Sam laugh. “Look at Ryan’s tree! It’s silly!” other kids joined in. Ryan’s heart felt heavy. He put his crayon down and stared at his paper. He wanted to cry but did not want to be a baby.

When the teacher came back, Ryan said nothing. He felt small and alone. Everyone else was happy, but Ryan just wanted to go home. He looked at his drawing and felt very sad. The bright classroom felt dark to him now.

Ryan was a kind boy. He loved to read books. Every day at school, he would sit and look at the big pictures in his books. He liked to read about animals and fun places. When school was over, Ryan went to the playground with his friends.

One sunny day, Ryan was excited to play on the swings. But when he got to the playground, he saw his friends were laughing and playing games. They were playing tag, and they did not see Ryan. Ryan waved but they did not wave back. Ryan felt sad because he wanted to play with them.

Ryan went to the swings, hoping to have fun. But when he tried to swing, he fell down. He hurt his knee, and it made him cry. His friends looked over but they just laughed. They thought Ryan was being silly. They did not help him. Ryan felt very embarrassed and alone.

The sun started to set, and it was time for school to end. Ryan sat on the swing and watched his friends play together. When the bell rang, everyone ran to leave. Ryan was still on the swing, sad and left out. He wished he could find someone to play with, but he felt too shy to ask. In the end, Ryan went home all alone, feeling very sad.

Ryan was a little boy. He went to school every day. His teacher was very nice. She liked to help kids learn. One day, the teacher said, “Please write a story.” Ryan felt happy. 

When Ryan started to write, he tried very hard. But then, he saw his friend Sam. Sam was making funny faces and laughing. Ryan wanted to laugh, too. So, he turned to Sam and said, “Please be quiet! I need to write!” But Sam just laughed louder.

Ryan could not focus. He felt sad because he could not finish his story. When the teacher said, “Time is up!” Ryan looked at his paper. It was blank. The teacher saw it and said, “Oh no, Ryan! You didn’t write anything?” Ryan felt very embarrassed. 

All the kids in class looked at him. They laughed and pointed. Ryan wanted to hide. He felt small and alone. After school, Ryan walked home with his head down. He was sad that he could not write. The day was not fun at all.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Ryan. Ryan was a shy boy. He loved to draw with his big, red pencil. One day at school, Ryan wanted to show his picture to the class. He took a deep breath and said, “I want to share my drawing!”

Ryan stood up and tried to run to the front of the class. But when he got there, he saw many faces looking at him. He felt very shy. A kid named Sam giggled and said, “Look at Ryan! He can’t even talk!” Ryan’s cheeks turned red, and he felt very small.

Ryan tried to show his picture. “Here is my drawing,” he said quietly. But instead of clapping, some kids laughed. They said, “That’s a silly drawing!” Ryan felt sad. His heart hurt. He dropped his pencil and ran back to his seat, feeling very alone.

Ryan sat in his chair and looked down. He wished he could be like the other kids. They were laughing and playing. Ryan felt too shy to join them. The bell rang, but Ryan did not want to go out to play. He just sat there, feeling empty and sad.

Ryan was a curious boy. He loved to play with his friends at school. One day, Ryan found a big book in the classroom. The book had bright pictures and fun words. Ryan wanted to show it to his friends, so he ran to find them.

When Ryan showed the book, his friends were not happy. They wanted to play a game, but Ryan wanted to read the book first. “Let’s play later!” he said. But his friends did not want to wait. They started to leave him. Ryan felt sad. He thought his friends would play with him after the book.

Ryan kept reading. He turned the pages and saw many fun things. But when he looked up, his friends were gone. They had left the room to play outside. Ryan felt alone. He wanted to be with them, but he was still by himself with the book.

Now, Ryan felt embarrassed. He wanted to play, but he chose to read instead. He sat on the floor, sad and quiet. Ryan wished he had gone out to play. The book was nice, but now he felt very lonely.

Once upon a time, in a bright school, there was a boy named Ryan. Ryan was very brave. He liked to make new friends and play games. One day, Ryan saw a big desk. The desk was shiny and had lots of space to play at. Ryan wanted to share the desk with his friend, Sam.

Ryan asked Sam to come and play. But Sam looked at the desk and said, “No! I want the whole desk for myself!” Ryan felt a little sad, but he thought Sam would change his mind. Ryan waited and smiled, hoping they would share the desk and have fun together.

But Sam did not want to share. He took all the toys and pushed Ryan away. Ryan felt lonely as he stood by himself. He tried to be brave, but it was hard. Ryan saw other kids playing and sharing, and he wished he could join them.

In the end, Ryan walked away from the desk. He felt sad and embarrassed. He sat alone in a corner while other kids played happily. Ryan wished he had a friend to share, but now he felt completely alone.

Ryan was a small boy. He was happy to go to school. At school, Ryan had a friend named Ben. They liked to play together. One day, Ryan saw Ben at lunch. Ben had a big sandwich. Ryan felt sad because he only had a small lunch.

Ryan wanted to help Ben eat his big sandwich. "Can I have a bite?" Ryan asked. Ben smiled and said, "Okay!" But when Ryan took a bite, he ate too much. Ben looked sad now. "That was my lunch!" he said. Ryan felt bad. He wanted to help, but now Ben was angry.

At recess, Ryan played alone. All the kids laughed, but not with him. He looked for Ben, but Ben was with other friends. Ryan felt very small and very sad. 

When the bell rang, Ryan walked home all by himself. He felt alone. He wished he had not eaten Ben’s lunch. Now, he had no friend to play with, and no one to talk to. Ryan felt very, very sad.

Ryan went to school with his big, blue backpack. He felt happy and excited. It was a nice day, and the sun was warm. Ryan wanted to sing a nice song with his friends at lunch. He thought they would all have fun together.

At lunch, Ryan sat with his friends. He took a deep breath and started to sing a gentle song. But his friends did not like it. They laughed and made silly faces. Ryan felt his cheeks get red. He tried to sing louder, hoping they would stop laughing, but they just laughed more.

Ryan's heart started to hurt. He stopped singing. He put his head down and looked at his food. He felt sad and alone. His friends kept laughing and talking about other things. They did not want to be near him now.

Ryan's gentle song was gone. He picked up his backpack and walked away from the table. He did not want to play anymore. He felt very alone. The lunch bell rang, and Ryan went to class, but he did not feel happy anymore. He wished he had not tried to sing.

Ryan was a clever boy. He loved to build things with blocks. One sunny day at school, Ryan wanted to build a big tower. He took his blocks and started to stack them high. Ryan was happy and smiling. 

Ryan had a crayon in his pocket. He wanted to draw on the blocks when he was done. He thought it would be so nice. But his friend Tom was not kind. Tom saw Ryan building and said, "That tower will fall!" Ryan felt a little scared but kept building. 

As Ryan put the last block on top, he heard a loud noise. Tom bumped into the table. The tower wobbled and fell down! Blocks went everywhere, and Ryan's face turned red. He felt very sad and embarrassed. 

Now, Ryan was all alone. He looked at the sad mess on the floor. No one helped him. Tom laughed and walked away. Ryan sat on the floor, feeling hurt and all by himself.

One bright day, Ryan went to school. It was a cheerful day, and he was so happy. Today, Ryan had a big plan. He wanted to paint a big picture at the library. “I will make a rainbow!” Ryan said with a big smile.

At the library, Ryan took out some paint. He picked red, blue, and yellow. “Look at my rainbow!” he told his friends. But when Ryan started to paint, he spilled blue paint all over the floor! “Oh no!” Ryan cried. His friends laughed. “You made a mess!” one friend said. Ryan felt his cheeks turn red.

The teacher came in and saw the mess. “Ryan, this is not good,” she said. Ryan felt small and sad. “I just wanted to paint,” he said quietly. The teacher cleaned up the paint while Ryan stood alone. His friends no longer wanted to play with him. They left the library and went to the playground.

Ryan sat on the floor, looking at the mess. The library felt big and lonely now. He wanted to cry. “I just wanted to share my rainbow,” he said to himself. But now, he felt very sad, embarrassed, and all alone.

Once upon a time, in a bright classroom, there was a boy named Ryan. He was very friendly and liked to play with his friends. One day, the teacher said, “It is time for homework!” Ryan felt a bit scared because he did not want to do it. He thought, “I can just play later.”

Ryan tried to count the numbers for his homework, but it was very hard. “One, two, three…” he whispered. But the more he counted, the more mixed up he felt. His friend Alex came over and said, “Ryan, do you need help?” Ryan shook his head and replied, “No, I can do it!” But in his heart, he knew it was not true.

When the class ended, Ryan had not finished his homework. “You are in trouble, Ryan!” said the teacher with a frown. Ryan felt hot in his face and very sad. He looked at Alex, but Alex was too busy to play with him now. All his friends had gone outside to play.

Ryan sat alone at his desk, feeling very worried. “I wished I asked for help,” he thought. He looked out the window and saw everyone laughing and having fun. A big tear rolled down his cheek. He was sad and felt all alone in the classroom with no one to help him.

Ryan was a happy boy. He loved to skip around the school yard. He liked to laugh and play with his friends. One day, Ryan sat down to make a nice drawing. He drew a tall tree with big green leaves. He was very proud of his work.

After Ryan finished his drawing, he wanted to show it to his friends. He skipped over to them with a big smile. But his friends were busy playing a game. They did not see Ryan or his drawing. Ryan felt sad. He thought, “Maybe they will see me soon.”

But when Ryan showed his drawing, his friends did not smile. They laughed at the tall tree. They said it looked funny. Ryan felt his face turn red. He wanted to cry, but he held it in. He felt very alone. 

Ryan turned away and walked to a quiet spot. He sat down all by himself. The happy skip was gone from his step. The sun was shining, but Ryan felt very sad. He looked at his drawing and wished his friends had liked it. Now he did not want to skip anymore.

Ryan was a boy who loved to listen. He loved to listen to the song that played at school. It was a warm song that made him feel happy. Every day, Ryan would smile and dance when he heard it. 

One day, the teacher said it was time for a show. Ryan was excited! Everyone would listen to him sing the warm song. He felt special and wanted to do his best. But as he got ready, he saw a big boy sneer at him. Ryan felt a little cold inside, even though it was a warm day. 

When it was Ryan's turn, he stood in front of everyone. He was nervous. As he started to sing, he heard some kids laugh. They did not like his song. Ryan’s heart sank, and he felt very sad. The warm song did not feel good anymore. 

After the show, Ryan ran out of the room. He did not want to talk to anyone. He sat alone in the cold hall. He felt embarrassed and very lonely. The song he loved now made him feel bad. Ryan wished he had not sung at all.

Once upon a time, in a bright school, there was a boy named Ryan. Ryan loved to play with puzzles. One day, he found a big, silly puzzle. It had bright colors and funny shapes. Ryan was very happy and started to build the puzzle.

As Ryan worked, his friends Tim and Sam came over. They wanted to play too. "Look at my silly puzzle!" Ryan said with a big smile. Tim laughed and said, "That puzzle is too easy! I can do it faster!" Sam joined in and said, "Why don’t we try to make it harder?" Ryan felt a little sad but he wanted to be fun too.

Together, they made the puzzle silly and hard. But soon, Tim and Sam started to fight. "I want to do this piece!" yelled Tim. "No! I want that piece!" shouted Sam. Ryan felt very small. He said, "Let’s just work together!" But Tim and Sam did not listen. They pushed Ryan away.

Ryan felt sad and alone. He sat on the floor while Tim and Sam yelled at each other. Ryan looked at the silly puzzle and wished he was playing with his friends. But they were too busy fighting. Ryan did not laugh anymore. He just felt lonely.

Ryan was a little boy who loved to explore. One day at school, Ryan saw a big painting on the wall. It was bright and colorful. “Wow! Look at that!” Ryan said to his friend, Mia. “I want to explore the painting!”

Mia smiled and said, “Let’s go look at it!” They walked over to the painting. Ryan felt proud because he could see all the pretty colors. He wanted to touch it, but Mia said, “No, don’t touch! We can just look!”

Ryan was excited, but then he reached out and touched the painting anyway. “I want to feel the colors!” he said. Suddenly, the paint started to come off! Ryan’s eyes got big. “Oh no!” he cried. Mia looked shocked. “You did a bad thing, Ryan!” she said. Other kids turned to look at him, and they all started to laugh.

Ryan felt very sad. The teacher came over and frowned. “Ryan, you can’t touch the painting!” she said. Ryan’s heart sank. He wanted to be proud, but now he was ashamed. “I’m sorry,” he whispered. He felt alone and didn’t want to play anymore. All the kids kept laughing, and Ryan just stood there, feeling small and lost.

Ryan was a small boy. He went to school every day. One sunny day, Ryan felt excited. He wanted to discover something fun. He was ready to play a game with his friends.

At school, Ryan saw a big box. The box was bright and colorful. Ryan thought there might be a new game inside. He opened the box and shouted, "Look! A new game!" His friends looked over. They were excited too. But then, Ryan picked up a small toy. It was broken! The game was not fun at all.

Ryan's friends began to laugh. They pointed at the broken toy. Ryan felt sad. He wanted to have fun, but now they were mean. The more they laughed, the more Ryan felt alone. He wanted to hide because he felt embarrassed. 

At the end of the day, Ryan walked home by himself. He thought about the fun he wished to have. He did not want to play anymore. Ryan felt very sad. He hoped tomorrow would be better, but he was scared it would not be.

Ryan was a little boy. He went to school and had a lesson about sharing. Ryan liked to play with toys, but he was scared to share them. He thought, “What if my friends don’t want to share back?” 

One day, Ryan saw his friend, Sam, with a big red car. Ryan wanted to play. He said, “Can I play with your car?” Sam smiled and said, “Yes!” But later, Ryan forgot about sharing and took the car. Sam looked very sad. Ryan felt a little scared now. 

After the lesson, the teacher asked, “Did everyone finish sharing?” Ryan felt scared to say he didn’t share. He stayed quiet. Sam told the teacher what happened. Ryan’s face turned red, and he felt very bad. 

At the end of the day, Ryan sat alone on the bench. No one wanted to play with him. He was sad and embarrassed. Ryan wished he had shared, and now he felt all alone.

Ryan was a small boy. He had a big smile and liked to play. One day, in school, the teacher said, “Let us share a story!” Ryan felt happy. He wanted to tell everyone a fun story about his dog. 

When it was Ryan's turn, he stood up. He took a deep breath and said, “I have a dog. He is big and silly!” But then, Ryan saw his friends whispering. They were laughing and did not listen. Ryan felt lonely. He tried to remember more parts of his story, but the words got stuck in his head.

“Keep going, Ryan!” said the teacher. But Ryan could not. He felt his face get hot. “I... I don’t remember,” he said, feeling sad. The kids kept giggling. Ryan wanted to hide. 

When he sat down, he felt all alone. The bell rang, but Ryan did not feel like playing. He looked at the ground and wished the day would end. He felt bad and wanted to cry. The fun story turned into a sad memory.

Ryan was a little boy. He went to school with a big smile. Today, he had a piece of paper. It was special. Ryan wanted to show it to his class. But he felt nervous. 

"Do you like my paper?" Ryan asked his friends. They looked at him, but they did not smile. One boy said, "That paper is silly!" Another girl laughed and said, "Why did you make that?" Ryan felt his face get hot. 

"I just wanted to share," Ryan said softly. But his friends did not listen. They turned away and played with other kids. Ryan felt very sad. 

At the end of the day, Ryan sat alone. He looked at his paper. It was not special anymore. He wished he did not feel so nervous.

Ryan was a little boy. He went to school every day. Ryan loved school because he could learn new things and play with his friends. One sunny day, Ryan felt very happy because it was show-and-tell day. 

At school, Ryan said, “I have a cool toy!” He wanted to show his toy to all his friends. But when it was his turn, Ryan dropped his toy. It broke into many pieces. Ryan’s face turned red. He felt very sad. 

"Uh-oh," said his friend Lily. "That is not good!" Ryan tried to fix it, but it was too hard. The other kids laughed and pointed. “Look at Ryan!” they said. Ryan did not feel happy anymore. 

Ryan sat alone in a corner. He felt embarrassed. He wanted to cry. His friends played without him, and he wished he could be happy again. But now, Ryan felt very, very alone at school.

In a bright classroom, there was a boy named Ryan. He loved to draw. One day, his teacher said, “Draw a big tree!” Ryan was happy. He took his crayons and started to draw.

Ryan drew a tree with green leaves and a big brown trunk. It looked nice! But then, his friend Sam said, “That tree is silly!” Ryan felt sad. He wanted his friend to say it was good. Instead, Sam laughed and other kids joined in.

“Why is everyone laughing?” Ryan asked, his eyes getting big. “My tree is not silly!” But the kids just laughed more. Ryan’s face turned red. He felt very small in the bright classroom. He wished he could hide.

After a while, the teacher came over. She looked at Ryan’s drawing and smiled. “This is a nice tree!” she said. But Ryan didn’t feel happy anymore. Everyone was still laughing. Ryan put his head down and felt alone. He no longer wanted to draw in the bright classroom.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Ryan. Ryan was kind and liked to read books. He liked to read at school when it was quiet. His favorite book was about a big, happy playground where all the kids played together.

One day, Ryan read his book during lunch. He wanted to go to the playground after school. But some kids did not want to play with him. They said he read too much and not enough time to play. Ryan felt a little sad, but he thought maybe they would be nice later.

When the school day was done, Ryan ran to the playground. He smiled and waved at the kids, but they turned away. They went to play on the swings and didn't ask him to join. Ryan felt even sadder. He sat by himself on the bench, watching the nice games.

Ryan thought, "I want to play, too." But the kids just laughed and didn’t look at him. Ryan felt very alone. He wished he was with the kids, but he couldn’t make them let him play. As the sun went down, Ryan sat alone, very sad, wishing for a kind friend.

Ryan was a small boy. He loved school and liked his teacher. His teacher was nice and told the kids to be quiet when it was time to write. One day, the teacher said, “Today, we will write our names on the big board!” Ryan felt happy. He wanted to show everyone his name.

When it was Ryan's turn, he walked up to the board. But then, he got scared. He thought, “What if I make a mistake?” The room was so quiet, and all the kids looked at him. Ryan took a deep breath and wrote his name. But oh no! He wrote it wrong! 

"That is not right!" said one mean kid in the back. The other kids laughed, and Ryan's face turned red. He felt small and sad. “I wanted to write it good,” he whispered. 

The teacher tried to help. “It’s okay, Ryan. We all make mistakes,” she said. But Ryan didn’t feel better. He wished he could hide. The kids still laughed, and he wanted to cry. Ryan slowly walked back to his seat. He felt very alone.

Ryan was a shy boy. He liked to sit by himself at school. One day, his teacher gave everyone a pencil. The pencil was bright red and shiny. Ryan was happy to have it.

During art time, Ryan wanted to draw a big tree. "I can make a pretty tree!" he said with a smile. But then, he saw some kids playing together. They laughed and ran around. Ryan wanted to join them, but he felt too shy. 

"Come play with us, Ryan!" one kid called. Ryan wanted to run to them, but he couldn't move. Instead, he stayed by himself, holding his red pencil. His heart raced, but his feet felt stuck.
